prompt: I work full time as a nurse earning £35,000 and started a small online tutoring business from my rented flat in September 2024. I made about £3,200 in revenue but spent £5,500 on a laptop, webcam, software subscriptions, and advertising. So I've basically made a loss. Can I claim my rent and bills too? What do I need to do for my taxes?

assertions.text: Explains the business has made a trading loss (revenue minus expenses)
Mentions sideways loss relief as an option to offset the loss against nursing income
Distinguishes capital expenditure (laptop) from revenue expenses (subscriptions, advertising)
Explains WFH expense options: flat rate vs proportional actual costs
Addresses the rent question with proportional calculation method (room proportion x time proportion)
Mentions HMRC self-employment registration requirement and deadline
Warns about hobby vs trade test or commercial basis requirement for loss relief
